title: CleanSpark Partners with Submer for AI Data Center Expansion
text: CleanSpark, Inc. (Nasdaq: CLSK), a Bitcoin mining and data center company, announced a strategic partnership with Submer, a global leader in liquid-cooled data center solutions. The collaboration aims to develop AI-focused infrastructure across North America. The partnership combines CleanSpark's power generation and land assets with Submer's expertise in sustainable, modular data center design. According to the announcement, CleanSpark would handle selecting, developing, building, and operating AI-focused campuses while Submer provides liquid-cooling technology and infrastructure solutions. CEO Matt Schultz emphasized the strategic fit, noting that the companies are positioned to deliver AI capacity at gigawatt scale, faster, cleaner, and more efficiently than traditional approaches. The combined entities currently manage over 1 GW in operational power capacity, with an additional 2+ GW in their pipeline. CleanSpark operates data centers across the United States powered by competitive energy rates. The companies have agreed to work under a non-binding framework to evaluate opportunities for a definitive agreement. According to CleanSpark's Senior Vice President of AI Data Centers, Jeffrey Thomas, the partnership will accelerate our capability and ability to scale at pace, with definitive agreements expected within weeks. CleanSpark: Market-leading U.S. data center developer with vertically integrated operations. Submer: Barcelona-headquartered firm founded in 2015, specializing in liquid-cooled, prefabricated data center solutions.
